response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable senior individual contributor with solid backend and distributed-systems experience, but he is fundamentally not a CTO candidate for this role. He holds no executive, VP, or engineering-director titles, has no demonstrated track record of building or scaling engineering organizations, and his AI experience is recent and at a contributor level (MCP tooling, agentic workflows) rather than architectural leadership over production multi-agent systems. His fintech exposure is limited to a single mid-level role at RIDE Capital with no evidence of credit-system or payment-infrastructure ownership. The gap between his profile and the role's hard requirements — 5+ years senior technical leadership, team scaling from 10 to 30+, production agentic AI at consequence-bearing commercial scale, board-level communication — is too large to bridge.
strengths: 17+ years of polyglot backend engineering across microservices, event-driven systems, and high-throughput infrastructure (Dunnhumby: millions of req/s, low-latency DLang services)
Current hands-on exposure to agentic AI workflows and MCP framework development at re:cinq, providing genuine first-principles familiarity with the tooling space
Consistent software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles, indicating strong engineering-culture instincts
concerns: Zero executive or organizational leadership experience — no team-building, headcount scaling, or board/investor communication track record anywhere in a 17-year career
AI credentials are early-stage contributor work (MCP generators, agentic-workflow tooling), not production multi-agent system architecture with financial or operational consequences
Fintech depth is superficial — one role at RIDE Capital as a developer with no ownership of credit systems, payment flows, risk modeling, or regulatory compliance infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Agentic AI at contributor not architect level
